examples.output: If attendance drops below three members for two consecutive sessions, or at the end of the pilot period in December 2026.

aiGuidance.extraction: Look for statements about when to revisit, what would change the decision, or what review conditions were set. Prefer condition-based triggers, which may reference a date as an anchor.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Avoid bare dates without a condition — revisit in December is weaker than revisit if attendance consistently drops or at the end of the pilot period. Dates can anchor a condition but should not replace one.
aiGuidance.purpose: Names the condition or event that should trigger a review of this decision — tied to the underlying reasoning so the review moment is meaningful.

description: The condition or event that would prompt a review of this decision.
name: revisit_when
